CREATE TABLE test_table1 (id INT, name VARCHAR(255), age INT);
CREATE TABLE test_table2 (id INT, test_table1_id INT, score DECIMAL(5,2), FOREIGN KEY (test_table1_id) REFERENCES test_table1(id));
INSERT INTO test_table1 (id, name, age) VALUES (1, 'Alice', 30);
INSERT INTO test_table1 (id, name, age) VALUES (2, 'Bob', 25);
INSERT INTO test_table2 (id, test_table1_id, score) VALUES (1, 1, 88.50);
INSERT INTO test_table2 (id, test_table1_id, score) VALUES (2, 1, 92.75);
INSERT INTO test_table2 (id, test_table1_id, score) VALUES (3, 2, 76.00);
SELECT t1.name, t2.score FROM test_table1 t1 JOIN test_table2 t2 ON t1.id = t2.test_table1_id WHERE t1.age > 20;
SELECT name FROM test_table1 WHERE id = (SELECT test_table1_id FROM test_table2 WHERE score = (SELECT MAX(score) FROM test_table2));
SELECT t1.name, AVG(t2.score) FROM test_table1 t1 LEFT JOIN test_table2 t2 ON t1.id = t2.test_table1_id GROUP BY t1.name HAVING AVG(t2.score) > 80;
UPDATE test_table1 SET age = age + 1 WHERE id = 1;
SELECT t1.name, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table2 t2 WHERE t2.test_table1_id = t1.id) AS score_count FROM test_table1 t1;
